methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Enovix (NASDAQ: ENVX) is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised over $700M to date. The comparable universe for this company is constrained: within Climate & Energy, there is 1 public peer (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 identifiable private peers including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. However, the peer-median method is not applicable here due to insufficient narrow peer density—only 2 same-stage comparables were identified, with zero adjacent-stage and zero similar-tier matches, falling short of the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints required for a reliable median calculation.

The inability to construct a tight peer-median set reflects the heterogeneity of Climate & Energy technologies and business models. While 19 total same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the filters for stage-adjacency or valuation-tier similarity. Attempting to apply an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misalignment with Enovix's specific technology and capital intensity.

Given the structural limitations of peer-median methodology here, valuation support would require alternative triangulation methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture-backed precedent transactions, or sector-specific revenue/EBITDA multiples—none of which were provided in this dataset. Without those inputs, no quantitative valuation range can be responsibly derived. Key uncertainties include technology risk, path to profitability, and the absence of tightly comparable trading or exit benchmarks.
